title: verbascum × carinthiacum
intro: Verbascum × carinthiacum, also known as Austrian mullein, is a hybrid plant species within the Verbascum genus, characterized by its large, hairy leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Verbascum × carinthiacum include reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and promoting wound healing, due to its high concentration of mucilages and saponins. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often in combination with other herbs, to utilize its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
